description: Fault propagation rules with multi-granularity injection support

rules._comment: ===== Container → Pod → Service → Span Multi-hop (for Container Resource Faults) =====

rules.name: RULE_CONTAINER_RESOURCE_TO_SPANS
rules.description: Container HIGH_CPU/HIGH_MEMORY affects all spans of the service via multi-hop path. Resource stress can cause latency, errors, or even missing spans.

rules.name: RULE_CONTAINER_RESTART_TO_SPANS
rules.description: Container RESTARTING causes missing spans via multi-hop path

rules._comment: ===== Pod → Service → Span Multi-hop (for Pod Lifecycle Faults) =====

rules.name: RULE_POD_RESOURCE_TO_SPANS
rules.description: Pod HIGH_CPU/HIGH_MEMORY affects all spans of the service

rules.name: RULE_SERVICE_DELAY_TO_SPAN
rules.description: Service delay propagates to span latency (first hop). Usage: 81,043

rules.name: RULE_SERVICE_ERROR_TO_SPAN
rules.description: Service errors propagate to span errors (first hop). Included for completeness even with 0 usage.

rules.name: RULE_SPAN_HIGH_LATENCY_TO_CALLER
rules.description: Latency propagates through call chain. Usage: 191,012 (PRIMARY RULE)

rules.name: RULE_SPAN_ERROR_TO_CALLER
rules.description: Errors propagate through call chain. Usage: 6,573

rules.name: RULE_SPAN_MISSING_TO_CALLER_ERROR
rules.description: Missing spans (e.g., from container restart) propagate as errors. Usage: 14,691

rules.name: RULE_SPAN_ERROR_BYPASS_HEALTHY_CONTROLLER
rules.description: Errors bypass healthy controller spans (Java OTel quirk). Usage: 32,311

rules.name: RULE_SPAN_ERROR_TO_CALLER_LATENCY
rules.description: Errors/timeouts cause upstream latency while waiting. Usage: 8 (new rule)

rules.name: RULE_SPAN_INJECTION_AFFECTED_TO_CALLER
rules.description: Injection-affected spans propagate errors/latency to callers. For infrastructure faults (network, container, pod, dns) where injection point spans may not show detectable anomalies.

rules.name: RULE_SPAN_HEALTHY_INJECTION_BYPASS_TO_ERROR
rules.description: For JVM method faults: injection span (controller, no error metrics) propagates through spans to reach caller HTTP spans with errors. Allows healthy/unknown src to bypass intermediate spans.

rules._comment: ===== Span → Pod → Span (JVM Stress Resource Contention via Pod) =====

rules.name: RULE_SPAN_JVM_STRESS_TO_SIBLING_VIA_POD
rules.description: JVM stress span affects sibling spans via pod resource contention. Requires pod to have HIGH_MEMORY/HIGH_CPU state to validate resource contention causality.

notes.optimization: Multi-granularity injection support - rules for container, pod, and service starting points
version_2.3_changes: Added span_009: INJECTION_AFFECTED spans propagate to callers for infrastructure faults
version_2.2_changes: Added first_hop_config to service_001 and service_002 for explicit first-hop validation
version_2.1_changes: Added container_001: Container resource faults to spans via multi-hop
Added container_002: Container restart to missing spans via multi-hop
Added pod_001: Pod resource faults to spans via multi-hop
removed_from_v2.0-streamlined: network_001, network_002: Pod network faults (0 usage)
disk_004, disk_005, disk_006: Pod disk faults (0 usage)
stress_003, stress_004: Pod resource competition (0 usage)

architecture.preprocessor: Container RESTARTING → Span MISSING_SPAN (before rule propagation)
architecture.injection_point_enhancer: Infrastructure faults → Mark injection service spans as INJECTION_AFFECTED
architecture.first_hop_service: Service[injection] → Span (service_001/002) - uses first_hop_config for lenient matching
architecture.first_hop_span: Span[injection] → Caller Spans (span_003/004 as first hop for HTTP faults)
architecture.propagation_source_note: HTTP Response fault handling uses fault_category in StartingPointResolver, not rule-level propagation_source

notes.first_hop_config_note: Service rules use first_hop_config with lenient matching since service is a dummy aggregation node without observable states
notes.service_002_note: Kept despite 0 usage for completeness - captures first-hop error propagation to spans
